title: How the Statements Connect
event: Ortiz Co. reports revenues of $120,000 and expenses of $85,000 for the year. Retained earnings began the year at $40,000 and dividends of $10,000 were declared and paid. Compute net income and ending retained earnings.

computationPaths.narration: Income statement feeds the retained earnings statement, which feeds the balance sheet. One river, three lakes.

memorize.body: Ending RE = Beginning RE + Net income − Dividends
Dividends are NOT an expense — they never touch net income. They come out of retained earnings directly.

questions.prompt: Beg RE $40,000 · NI $35,000 · dividends $10,000. Ending retained earnings = ?

distractors.feedback: You forgot the dividends. They reduce retained earnings even though they skip the income statement: 40 + 35 − 10 = 65.
You dropped the beginning balance. RE rolls forward — start with last year's 40,000.
That's net income alone. The question asked for ending RETAINED EARNINGS, the running total.
